source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Explosion - MC 109 A Platform

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Mississippi Canyon Block 109

actions_taken: Night operators investigated the explosion source
Person In Charge notified and investigated next morning
Platform shut in at 0800 due to unrelated high pipeline pressure
American Midstream personnel arrived at 1000 to investigate
Inspectors performed walkthrough and photographed damage
event.summary: An explosion occurred on 14 February 2019 at 0115 on the Mississippi Canyon 109 A (Amberjack) Platform. The explosion originated from a 12 Volt battery located inside a Remote Terminal Unit (RTU) Panel used for gas sales measurement and communication. A rechargeable battery had been overcharged due to improper replacement and installation in a non-vented enclosure, causing hydrogen gas accumulation and ignition of electrical components. The panel door was blown off and debris landed 45 feet away. No injuries or pollution occurred. The root cause was flawed equipment design and installation of non-rated electrical components in a hazardous area, compounded by inadequate personnel training on battery hydrogen venting and lack of hazard analysis during design changes.
recommendations: The New Orleans District recommends that the Office of Incident Investigations considers drafting a Safety Alert regarding electrical components and hazardous areas
key_phrases: electrical components not rated for hazardous area

hazards.name: Hydrogen gas accumulation in enclosed spaces
Electrical ignition sources in hazardous areas

threats.name: Rechargeable battery installed in non-vented enclosure
Improper battery replacement with incompatible type
Incorrect charging voltage applied to battery
Electrical components not rated for Class 1 Division 2 area
Hydrogen gas ignition by unrated electrical components

controls.name: Vented enclosure design for rechargeable batteries
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hydrogen accumulation in enclosed spaces
controls.lod_basis: API RP 14F and API RP 500 requirements for battery installation

supporting_text: The installation of rechargeable batteries inside a non-vented panel creates a Class 1 Division 1 location inside of the panel according to API RP 500
All rechargeable battery systems should be installed such that hydrogen cannot collect in sufficient quantity to create a hazard

controls.name: Use of NRTL-listed electrical components for hazardous areas
controls.barrier_role: Prevent ignition of hydrogen in hazardous classified areas
controls.lod_basis: API RP 500 and 30 CFR 250.114(c) requirements for electrical installations in hazardous areas

supporting_text: BSEE inspectors noted that the electrical components inside the panel were not listed by a Nationally Recognized Testing Laboratory (NRTL) for use in a Class 1 Division 2 hazardous area
Electrical components inside the RTU Panel were not rated for the Class 1 Division 2 area in which the panel was installed

controls.name: Proper battery selection and compatibility verification
controls.barrier_role: Prevent installation of incompatible battery types
controls.lod_basis: Equipment maintenance and design change procedures

supporting_text: The electrician replaced two 12V Odyssey batteries with one 12V ACDELCO Voyager II Marine/RV Battery
Eliminating one battery resulted in directing the 24V charge into the one 12V battery causing a hazardous overcharge

controls.name: Hazard analysis for design changes to electrical systems
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards before implementing equipment modifications
controls.lod_basis: Management of Change and Hazard Analysis procedures

supporting_text: Personnel failed to perform a Hazard Analysis before changing the design of the RTU Panel
No or Inadequate Hazard Analysis listed as contributing cause

controls.name: Personnel training on battery hydrogen venting hazards
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operators understand hydrogen venting from rechargeable batteries
controls.lod_basis: API RP 14F requirements and personnel competency

supporting_text: BSEE Investigators found that the operators did not have knowledge of the battery's ability to vent hydrogen within the panel
Personnel Poorly Trained: Personnel failed to recognize that rechargeable batteries vent hydrogen

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown (ESD) activation and muster procedures
controls.barrier_role: Mitigate consequences by shutting down operations and evacuating personnel

supporting_text: The Night Operators chose not to activate the Emergency Shut Down (ESD), shut in production, or muster
Personnel searched but could not identify a gas source

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
